name: Mail Pipeline
description: Real-time view of queue, worker, decision, and persistence steps for mail processing.

nodes.label: Email Account Connected
nodes.description: External mailbox connection trigger that starts the mail pipeline.
notes: Stores Gmail auth state in mail_oauth_tokens.
Resets the backfill cursor and kicks off the backfill start job.

nodes.label: Backfill Queue
notes: Holds both backfill start jobs and backfill chunk jobs.
Uses mail_cursors.backfill_page_token to control paging.
An empty token starts from the first page; a saved token resumes from that page.
A done marker skips future fetch/enqueue for the mailbox.

nodes.label: Backfill Worker
nodes.description: Backfills historical mail for newly connected accounts.
notes: Consumes both backfill start jobs and backfill chunk jobs.
Fetches historical threads, stores raw payloads, records thread metadata, and hands off extract work.

nodes.label: Cron Scheduler
nodes.description: Recurring scheduler boundary that decides when mailbox checks should run.

nodes.label: Enqueue Cron Tick
nodes.description: Queues the recurring cron tick that drives mailbox checks every minute.

nodes.label: Incoming Queue
notes: Holds both the recurring mailbox-sweep work and mailbox incoming-check work.
Jobs can arrive from multiple vendors and mailboxes.

nodes.label: Incoming Worker
nodes.description: Checks connected inboxes for new mail and hands off downstream work.
notes: Picks up both scheduled mailbox-sweep work and mailbox incoming-check work from the Incoming Queue.
If no incoming_history_id exists yet, incoming processing writes the baseline cursor and stops.

nodes.label: Schedule Incoming Checks
nodes.description: Finds mailboxes that should get an incoming check and prepares the next queue work.
notes: Reads connected mailbox rows from mail_oauth_tokens.
Looks up mail_cursors.incoming_check_scheduled_at to find mailboxes due for a check.

nodes.label: Enqueue Incoming Check
nodes.description: Queues a mailbox incoming check when unread mail should be processed.

nodes.label: Write Schedule Cursor
nodes.description: Updates the scheduled incoming-check cursor timestamp in Postgres.
notes: Writes mail_cursors.incoming_check_scheduled_at in Postgres.
Marks when the mailbox was last scheduled for an incoming check.

nodes.label: Persist Incoming Mail
nodes.description: Stores incoming thread payloads, records normalized thread state, and queues follow-on work.
notes: Incoming path only.
Stores raw thread artifacts in the thread store.
Writes mail_threads rows with s3_key, content_hash, subject, participants, and ingest timestamp.
Queues extract and analyze follow-on work for eligible incoming threads.

nodes.label: Persist Backfill Mail
nodes.description: Stores historical thread payloads, records normalized thread state, and queues follow-on work.
notes: Backfill path only.
Stores raw thread artifacts in the thread store.
Writes mail_threads rows with s3_key, content_hash, subject, participants, and ingest timestamp.
Queues extract follow-on work for eligible historical threads.

nodes.label: Advance Backfill Cursor
nodes.description: Updates the backfill cursor state in Postgres.
notes: Writes the next mail_cursors.backfill_page_token value or the done marker.
When another page exists, this step also schedules the next backfill chunk job.

nodes.label: S3
nodes.description: Thread artifact store for raw mailbox thread data.
notes: Shared thread artifact store. Duplicated visually in this lane for readability — the backfill lane writes to the same bucket.
Receives raw incoming thread artifacts keyed per stored mailbox thread payload.

nodes.label: Postgres
nodes.description: Primary relational store for mail auth, cursors, and thread state.
notes: Shared Postgres store. Duplicated visually in this lane for readability — the backfill lane writes to the same database.
Stores mailbox auth state in mail_oauth_tokens, incoming and backfill cursors in mail_cursors, and normalized thread state in mail_threads.

nodes.label: S3
nodes.description: Thread artifact store for raw mailbox thread data.
notes: Shared thread artifact store. Duplicated visually in this lane for readability — the incoming lane writes to the same bucket.
Receives raw historical thread artifacts keyed per stored mailbox thread payload.

nodes.label: Postgres
nodes.description: Primary relational store for mail auth, cursors, and thread state.
notes: Shared Postgres store. Duplicated visually in this lane for readability — the incoming lane writes to the same database.
Stores mailbox auth state in mail_oauth_tokens, incoming and backfill cursors in mail_cursors, and normalized thread state in mail_threads.

nodes.label: Advance Incoming Cursor
nodes.description: Updates the incoming history cursor after downstream queue handoff succeeds.
notes: Incoming path only.
Writes mail_cursors.incoming_history_id in Postgres.
Skips the write when no next_cursor is returned.

nodes.label: Analyze Queue
notes: Holds handle_analyze_reply jobs for run-start threads.

nodes.label: Analyze Worker
nodes.description: Analyzes extracted mail and decides whether to draft a reply.

nodes.label: Analyze Prechecks
nodes.description: Loads the stored thread and clears the early stop or batch-reuse checks before the LLM branch runs.
notes: Stops if the latest sender is the mailbox owner.
Reuses an active action batch for the same thread/content when one already exists.

nodes.label: Next Action?
nodes.description: Runs propose-mode analysis to choose the next thread action after prechecks pass.
notes: The LLM can return skip, needs_review, or draft_reply.
Main analyze result telemetry lands here.

nodes.label: Skip Internal Sender
nodes.description: Stops analyze when the latest sender is the mailbox owner.

nodes.label: Reuse Existing Batch
nodes.description: Reuses the latest active action batch and updates thread reply state from that batch.

nodes.label: Mark Skipped
nodes.description: Writes mail_threads.reply_status = skipped and ends this branch.

nodes.label: Needs Review
nodes.description: Falls back to needs_review when analyze returns an error, malformed output, or draft-generation fallback.
notes: Some needs-review outcomes happen without a draft ever being created.

nodes.label: Draft Reply
nodes.description: Generates the reply draft and writes the reply_drafts row before approval or send decisions continue.
notes: If supported proposed actions exist, draft status becomes approval_pending and an action batch is created.
If supported actions are missing or unsupported, draft status becomes needs_review.

nodes.label: Auto Send?
nodes.description: Checks whether autosend is enabled and the action batch can be auto-approved.
notes: Human approval and auto-approval both converge on the same approved-actions execution queue.

nodes.label: Pause Approval
nodes.description: Stops the flow until someone manually approves the pending action batch.

nodes.label: Approve Batch
nodes.description: Manual approval path that approves the batch and enqueues the approved-actions job.

nodes.label: Auto Approve
nodes.description: Auto-approves the batch and enqueues the approved-actions job.
notes: Reverts the batch to pending approval if the execute-approved enqueue fails.

nodes.label: Actions Queue
notes: Holds execute-approved-actions jobs from both auto approval and manual approval.

nodes.label: Actions Worker
nodes.description: Claims the approved batch, executes pending actions, and prepares the send handoff.
notes: If action execution fails, the batch is marked failed before send is ever queued.

nodes.label: Run Approved Actions
nodes.description: Executes approved actions for the batch and prepares the send handoff.
notes: Only successful execution continues to the send queue handoff.

nodes.label: Queue Send
nodes.description: Enqueues send-reply work after approved actions complete successfully.
notes: This is the boundary into the next send section.

nodes.label: Action Failed
nodes.description: Marks the action batch as failed and updates reply state before send is skipped.
notes: Some failures also recover the draft back to needs review.

nodes.label: Extract Queue
notes: Receives handle_mail_extract jobs from both incoming and backfill persistence steps.
Each job carries stored thread lineage such as thread_id, s3_key, content_hash, and run_id.
AI provider credentials are required for extract execution.

nodes.label: Extract Worker
nodes.description: Runs thread extraction and persists durable extract results from stored mail.
notes: Owns AI extraction, durable contact persistence, contact-thread insight persistence, and final extract state recording.

nodes.label: Run Extract
nodes.description: Loads the stored thread payload and runs one AI extract pass for contacts and thread insight.

nodes.label: Extract Succeeded?
nodes.description: Checks whether the extract step produced a usable result.

nodes.label: Retry Extract
nodes.description: Records extract error state and returns a retryable job error.
notes: Used for extract execution failures and durable contact persistence failures.

nodes.label: Persist Extract Results
nodes.description: Writes durable extract results after a successful AI extract.
notes: Upserts normalized contact rows into mail_extracted_contacts.
Persists contact-thread insights after the durable contact upsert succeeds.
Contact-thread insight and persist-sink failures warn and continue without retry.
A durable contact upsert failure returns a retryable job error.

nodes.label: Postgres
notes: Stores durable extract rows in mail_extracted_contacts.
Stores contact-thread insights for the thread after contact persistence succeeds.
Stores thread extract state such as customers_count and final extract error.

nodes.label: Record Extract State
nodes.description: Records final extract summary on the thread after durable extract persistence.
notes: Writes customers_count and final extract state to thread state storage.
This write is best-effort; failures are logged without retrying the whole extract job.

nodes.label: Send Worker
nodes.description: Picks up queued send-reply jobs and hands off to the send lifecycle.
notes: This worker owns the send precheck telemetry before the send lifecycle finalizes the result.

nodes.label: Send Reply
nodes.description: Claims the draft, validates send prerequisites, calls the provider, and finalizes the outcome.
notes: Validates thread state, Gmail send scope, recipient, access token, and normalized body before the provider call.
No-ops if the draft is already approval_pending, sent, or terminal send_failed.

nodes.label: Mark Sent
nodes.description: Marks both the draft and thread as sent after the provider call succeeds.

nodes.label: Terminal Non-Send
nodes.description: Records a terminal non-send outcome and updates draft and thread state.
notes: Covers stale drafts, missing auth or scope, invalid recipient, empty body, and terminal provider failures.
Reply status becomes stale or send_failed depending on the cause.

nodes.label: Mark Needs Review
nodes.description: Moves the draft and thread back to needs_review after a retryable provider failure.
